_patch_meta.description: Dimension Review C13 Reg 30 (contrition): 9 groups assigned. Dominant dimension: Dependence / Creatureliness (5 groups). 1 Session D pointer (DIM-30-SD001). Registry stamp set. CLUSTER STAMP INCLUDED — this is the final C13 registry. All 10 registries now complete.

set.notes: Inner-being anguish from crushing — soul crushed to ground, sitting in darkness. AV Psa 143:3 names the inner desolation directly.

Substitutionary crushing of the Servant for iniquity — producing peace and healing. Genuinely dual subject — Servant's inner person and beneficiaries' transformation are inseparable. AV Isa 53:5 is precise.
The contrite and crushed inner spirit as condition of divine indwelling and revival. God dwelling with the lowly/contrite. AV Isa 57:15 is the defining verse for Registry 30.
Crushed and contrite inner spirit drawing divine nearness and salvation. AV Psa 34:18: Lord near to the brokenhearted, saves the crushed in spirit.
The broken and contrite heart as the inner sacrifice acceptable to God. AV Psa 51:17 — the sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; a broken and contrite heart he will not despise.
Corporate inner desolation under divine crushing — community broken by God, covered with shadow of death. AV Psa 44:19 confirms.

Humble and contrite spirit trembling at God's word — three inner postures (humble, contrite, trembling) receiving divine attention. AV Isa 66:2 is the fullest description of contrition in the registry.

Inner terror and panic in devastating defeat. Most peripheral group to contrition characteristic — broken inner state via defeat/terror rather than penitential brokenness. AV Jer 46:5 names dismay and terror explicitly.
Crushed/contrite inner spirit drawing divine nearness — homograph sub-entry of H1793A dak.ka. Same anchor verse (Psa 34:18). Same characteristic. See SBF-036-001 for Session B assessment.

record.description: Registry 30 (contrition) produces strong convergence: 5 of 9 groups assign Dependence / Creatureliness (7552-003, 7553-001, 7554-001, 7555-001, 7557-001). This convergence suggests contrition is the penitential sub-form of Dependence / Creatureliness — brokenness before God arising from moral self-awareness and recognition of unworthiness. Session D should examine whether the programme vocabulary needs a finer distinction between general dependence/trust and specifically penitential dependence (contrition). Connect with Reg 10 (dependence/trust) and Reg 80 (trust) in the programme.

operations.description: Session D pointer DIM-30-SD001: contrition as penitential sub-form of Dependence / Creatureliness
Registry 30 (contrition): Dimension Review complete under v2.2

record.notes: C13 cluster complete. 10 registries: condemnation (24), conscience (26), contrition (30), covetousness (35), disobedience (50), greed (70), guilt (73), justice (98), reconciliation (130), repentance (135). 144 groups total. Contrition reinstated from phase1_status=Excluded following researcher decision 2026-04-13. Cluster stamp deferred from initial session pending Reg 30 reinstatement — now set with this patch.

operations.description: C13 cluster stamp — Dimension Review complete for all 10 registries
